generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance StructuralHealthSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time sensor capturing structural integrity metrics (e.g., vibration, strain) for building components. Part of heterogeneous IoT infrastructure feeding operational data into the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralHealthDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor measuring environmental parameters (e.g., temperature, humidity, air quality) in real-time for building operations and predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted
]

instance OperationalLogSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Legacy system or IoT device capturing operational logs (e.g., HVAC status, elevator usage) for runtime performance tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalLogDataTransmitted
]

instance BimModelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Semantic extraction component translating BIM model attributes (e.g., geometric, spatial, functional) into runtime-compatible data streams."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BimModelDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance StructuralHealthDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthSensor
    base:desc "High-frequency structural health data stream (e.g., vibration, strain) synchronized via Eclipse Hono/Ditto for real-time Digital Twin updates."
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
    base:desc "Continuous environmental data stream (e.g., temperature, humidity) with near-real-time transmission for operational monitoring."
]

instance OperationalLogDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalLogSensor
    base:desc "Discrete operational logs (e.g., system alerts, usage metrics) transmitted asynchronously for historical and predictive analysis."
]

instance BimModelDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BimModelSensor
    base:desc "Semantically enriched BIM model data (e.g., IFC/BCF exports) converted into runtime-compatible format for Digital Twin integration."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BuildingInformationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically rich BIM model encapsulating geometric, spatial, and functional characteristics of building components (e.g., walls, HVAC systems) as per IFC/BCF standards."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimModelTransformationEnabler
]

instance RuntimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time data from sensors and operational logs, normalized for consistency across heterogeneous sources."
    DTDFVocab:fromData StructuralHealthDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted, OperationalLogDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler
]

instance EnrichedBimModelData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "BIM model enriched with runtime operational data (e.g., sensor annotations, performance metrics) for unified Digital Twin representation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BimModelDataTransmitted, RuntimeOperationalData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelEnrichmentEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BimModelTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) tool converting BIM models into deployable Digital Twin components using semantic transformation rules."
    DTDFVocab:enables BimIntegrationService
]

instance DataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono/Ditto-based framework ensuring real-time synchronization and semantic integration of heterogeneous data streams."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance ModelEnrichmentEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Semantic enrichment engine merging BIM models with operational data to create unified Digital Twin representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, OptimizationService
]

instance AnalyticsProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Modular analytics pipeline supporting machine learning, rule-based engines, and simulation tools for custom service integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Aggregates and visualizes runtime data (e.g., sensor inputs, environmental conditions) for operational state tracking and anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalStateInsight, AnomalyDetectionAction
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Leverages historical and real-time data to forecast asset degradation, failure risks, or energy consumption trends."
    DTDFVocab:provides DegradationForecastInsight, MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Applies algorithmic models (e.g., ML, simulation) to optimize resource allocation (energy, materials) and operational efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:provides ResourceAllocationInsight, EfficiencyOptimizationAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides interactive 3D/4D BIM-integrated representations for stakeholder collaboration and situational awareness."
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveVisualizationInsight
]

instance DecisionSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Facilitates data-driven decision-making with actionable insights derived from simulations and predictive models."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlLogicValidationInsight, CustomAnalyticsIntegrationAction
]

instance BimIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables seamless integration of BIM models with runtime data for lifecycle management and design coordination."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelConsistencyInsight, ClashDetectionAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OperationalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time system state insights (e.g., sensor readings, actuator status) for Facility Managers to monitor building performance."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated detection of operational anomalies (e.g., sensor deviations, system failures) triggering alerts."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DegradationForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive insights on asset degradation risks (e.g., structural wear, equipment failure) based on historical and real-time data."
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Proactive maintenance recommendations generated from predictive analytics for Building Control Engineers."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ResourceAllocationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized resource allocation recommendations (e.g., energy, materials) derived from simulation models."
]

instance EfficiencyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustments to operational parameters (e.g., HVAC settings) based on optimization algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ImmersiveVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D BIM overlays with real-time data for collaborative decision-making across stakeholders."
]

instance ControlLogicValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Semantic validation of Building Control System (BCS) logic against enriched BIM models for dynamic refinement."
]

instance CustomAnalyticsIntegrationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "DT Engineers deploy domain-specific analytics (e.g., predictive maintenance, energy optimization) via custom services."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ModelConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation of semantic consistency between BIM models and runtime data to ensure Digital Twin accuracy."
]

instance ClashDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated clash detection between BIM components and operational constraints during design coordination."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: System Under Study
instance AeCoInfrastructureDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital Twin system for Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations (AECO) infrastructure, integrating static BIM models with real-time operational data across building lifecycle stages."
    base:contains BuildingInformationModel, RuntimeOperationalData, EnrichedBimModelData,
                 StructuralHealthSensor, EnvironmentalConditionSensor, OperationalLogSensor,
                 BimModelSensor, BimModelTransformationEnabler, DataIntegrationEnabler,
                 ModelEnrichmentEnabler, AnalyticsProcessingEnabler,
                 RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService,
                 VisualizationService, DecisionSupportService, BimIntegrationService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DT system enables **real-time control commands** from the digital twin to physical building systems, executed by **Facility Managers** via the deployed DT interface. The **command types** are not explicitly detailed but are inferred to include **operational adjustments** (e.g., HVAC setpoints, lighting controls, or security system activations). **Control message formats** are likely standardized to ensure compatibility with building automation systems, though the document does not specify exact formats (e.g., JSON, XML, or proprietary protocols). **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are implied by the **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, which emphasize **interoperability and security**, suggesting role-based access control (RBAC) or cryptographic validation (e.g., digital signatures) to prevent unauthorized commands. **Feedback systems** are supported through **bidirectional data exchange**, where the physical system’s response (e.g., sensor confirmations, status updates) is relayed back to the DT for continuous synchronization. **Conflict resolution** is addressed via the **runtime configuration management** feature, allowing dynamic adjustments without full redeployment, though specific algorithms (e.g., priority-based or consensus-driven) are not described. **Command execution confirmation** is ensured through the **holistic monitoring and control loop**, where the DT validates the physical system’s state post-command execution. The system’s **modular architecture** enables custom services to extend control logic, though exact implementation details are not provided."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, implying sub-second to millisecond-level latency requirements for critical operational services. The framework supports dynamic temporal granularity tailored to service-specific needs, enabling adaptive synchronization frequencies (e.g., high-frequency updates for sensor-driven control systems and lower-frequency updates for static BIM model enrichment). Runtime configuration management further allows adjustments to synchronization parameters without full redeployment, ensuring alignment with evolving operational demands. The system prioritizes low-latency bidirectional data flow to maintain operational fidelity, particularly for **Building Control Engineers** and **Facility Managers** interacting with the deployed Digital Twin for monitoring and control."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated architecture pattern** centered on the **DT Framework**, which orchestrates interactions between enriched BIM models, operational Digital Twins, and external systems. The constellation follows a **service-oriented integration model**, where components (e.g., BIM tools, IoT sensors, analytics services) are modularly connected via standardized extension points. Resource management is handled dynamically through **runtime configuration management**, enabling adaptive scaling and reconfiguration of services. System-wide coordination is achieved via bidirectional data exchange, ensuring holistic building life cycle management from deployment to operational phases. The framework’s **modularity** and **standardized interfaces** facilitate seamless integration of heterogeneous components while maintaining interoperability."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **runtime operation**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling and Configuration**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** directly within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with **semantic representations** of Building Control System (BCS) components. This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Transformation and Artifact Generation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts **structured representations** from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** (e.g., simulation models, control logic, or integration scripts) with **minimal manual intervention**.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed to the physical infrastructure, enabling synchronization between the **physical building** and its **digital twin**. This phase includes **real-time data exchange capabilities** to maintain continuous synchronization.
  4. **Runtime Operation and Customization**: The DT operates in real-time, supporting **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring and control. The **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points** allows Facility Managers to interact with the twin (e.g., monitoring system states) and DT Engineers to integrate **custom services, plugins, or analytics** to extend functionality dynamically.
  5. **Lifecycle Governance**: The framework ensures **comprehensive governance** by aligning with use cases for **Building Control Engineers** (device modeling and control logic), **Facility Managers** (monitoring and operation), and **DT Engineers** (custom service development). Transition management between phases is facilitated by **automated workflows** and **semantic consistency checks** derived from MDE principles, ensuring traceability and adaptability throughout the DT’s operational life cycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **real-time synchronization** (via Eclipse Hono and Ditto) to minimize discrepancies between physical assets and their digital representations. Validation and verification methods are implied through the **model-driven deployment framework**, which ensures alignment between **design-time models (BIM)** and **runtime operations**. Uncertainty sources may arise from **data latency, sensor inaccuracies, or model abstraction gaps**, though explicit quantification or error correction mechanisms are not specified. Quality assurance processes are embedded in the **modular architecture**, which supports extensibility for domain-specific refinements, ensuring adaptability to AECO-specific fidelity requirements."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for this DT system is designed to support **interoperability across heterogeneous systems**, implying a **hybrid network topology** that may include **cloud, edge, and on-premise components**. While **communication protocols** are not explicitly named, the system’s emphasis on **real-time data exchange** suggests support for **low-latency protocols** (e.g., MQTT for IoT, OPC UA for industrial automation, or WebSockets for web-based interactions). The **network architecture** is likely **modular and service-oriented**, aligning with the framework’s **standardized extension points** for custom services. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are critical for real-time synchronization, though specific thresholds (e.g., <100ms) are not provided. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are implied by the **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, which prioritize **scalability and maintainability**, suggesting failover systems, load balancing, or distributed data storage. **Security protocols** for network communication are not detailed but are inferred to include **encryption (e.g., TLS), authentication (e.g., OAuth, JWT), and access control** to ensure data integrity and confidentiality across heterogeneous systems."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for this DT system supports **multi-platform hosting**, including **cloud, edge, and on-premise** environments, to accommodate varying operational needs. The **computational and storage requirements** are tailored to the **modular architecture**, enabling **scalability from individual components to entire infrastructure systems**. The system leverages **runtime configuration management** to allow **low-overhead updates**, suggesting **containerization (e.g., Docker) or microservices** for dynamic deployment adjustments. **Deployment models** are implied to include **hybrid cloud-edge-on-premise** configurations, with the DT Framework acting as a **central hub** for orchestration. **Availability and reliability assurance** are addressed through the **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, which emphasize **maintainability and scalability**, though specific SLAs (e.g., 99.9% uptime) are not provided. The system’s **modular design** allows for **incremental deployment**, where custom services can be integrated without full system redeployment, aligning with the **runtime adaptability** requirement. The **enriched BIM-to-DT workflow** (as shown in Figure 1) suggests that deployment begins with **BIM tools** (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) for device modeling, followed by **Facility Manager interaction** for operational use, indicating a **phased deployment approach**."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** as a core non-functional requirement (**NF_X**), ensuring seamless horizontal integration with external systems via standardized protocols and data exchange mechanisms. Integration patterns include **bidirectional data flow** between the DT Framework and external enterprise systems (e.g., BIM tools, IoT platforms, facility management software) using **modular extension points**. Dependencies on external systems are mitigated through the framework’s **runtime configuration management**, allowing low-overhead updates and adaptability. The system adheres to **interoperability standards** (implicitly referenced) to bridge static BIM representations with dynamic operational needs, supporting integration with other Digital Twins or enterprise-wide infrastructure systems while maintaining scalability and accessibility."
]
